text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," say "increase website traffic by 20% in three months." Once you have your main goal, break it down into smaller milestones, such as weekly tasks or monthly reviews. These help you track progress and stay on schedule. It is also important to define your deliverables—the specific results you will produce, like a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment. This means everyone involved agrees on the goals and understands their role. Without this, projects can easily lose direction.
